legs.label: Exchange booth
legs.notes: Reputable booths charge no commission — the rate is the whole story. Large, clean $100 notes get the best rate.

legs.label: Schwab debit · ATM
legs.subgroupNote: Same machine, different card — the card decides the cost. Thai bank ATMs charge ฿250 a pull (modeled at ฿250; Mastercard pays ฿350 at some banks).
legs.notes: Schwab Investor Checking: no foreign-transaction fee, and every ATM fee — including the Thai ฿250 — is rebated at month-end. You get Visa's wholesale rate, on average ~0.3% off mid. Needs the free brokerage + checking account opened before your trip.

legs.label: Wise card · ATM
legs.subgroupNote: Same machine, different card — the card decides the cost. Thai bank ATMs charge ฿250 a pull (modeled at ฿250; Mastercard pays ฿350 at some banks).
legs.notes: Wise's published US schedule: 0.57% USD→THB conversion, $250/month of fee-free ATM withdrawals, then $1.95 + 1.95% per withdrawal.

legs.label: Revolut card · ATM
legs.subgroupNote: Same machine, different card — the card decides the cost. Thai bank ATMs charge ฿250 a pull (modeled at ฿250; Mastercard pays ฿350 at some banks).
legs.notes: Revolut US Standard: 2% fee on every out-of-network ATM withdrawal (all Thai ATMs are). Exchanging over $1,000/month adds a 0.5% fair-usage fee, and converting outside market hours (Fri 5pm–Sun 6pm ET) adds 1% — convert before the weekend.

legs.label: Your debit card · ATM
legs.subgroupNote: Same machine, different card — the card decides the cost. Thai bank ATMs charge ฿250 a pull (modeled at ฿250; Mastercard pays ฿350 at some banks).
legs.notes: Modeled on your own bank's fees — set your foreign-transaction fee and ATM fee in Setup to make this exact.

legs.label: Your credit card · ATM
legs.subgroupNote: Same machine, different card — the card decides the cost. Thai bank ATMs charge ฿250 a pull (modeled at ฿250; Mastercard pays ฿350 at some banks).
legs.acceptanceNote: Amex / Discover often rejected at Thai ATMs
legs.notes: Withdrawing cash with a credit card is a cash advance: most issuers charge 5% (min $10) plus ~30% APR from day one — no grace period. Modeled assuming you pay it off in 30 days; set your card's actual fee in Setup.

legs.label: Wise
legs.notes: Converts at the mid-market rate; a real $1,000 quote on 2026-06-11 carried $9.65 in fees (~0.96%) — modeled as $0.80 + 0.885%. ACH-funded transfers take ~4–6 days end-to-end (that same quote: sent Thursday, arriving the next Wednesday); paying from a Wise balance or by wire is much faster. Debit funding adds 1%, credit 3%.

legs.label: Remitly
legs.notes: Economy bank deposit, no fee at this size, ~3–5 business days. The margin moves with promotions — measured ~0.4% on 2026-06-11. Express (card-funded) arrives in minutes but costs more.

legs.label: Revolut
legs.notes: Revolut US: 0.15% transfer fee (min $0.75), typically 1–3 business days to a Thai bank. Their THB rate carried a ~0.5% spread when measured 2026-06-11 (majors are tighter). Exchanging over $1,000/month adds 0.5%, and converting outside market hours (Fri 5pm–Sun 6pm ET) adds 1%.

legs.label: Xe
legs.notes: No transfer fee over $500 ($3 under); typically lands in 1–4 business days. The cost is the rate margin — measured ~1.0% USD→THB on 2026-06-11; Xe's markup varies 0.5–2%.

legs.label: OFX
legs.notes: No transfer fee, $150 minimum; typically 2–5 business days end-to-end. Retail margin typically 1–2% on exotics and improves a lot on large transfers — built for big moves; get their live quote above ~$10k.

booths.name: SuperRich Thailand
booths.areas: 45 Soi Ratchadamri 1 HQ · counters at major BTS stops
booths.note: The benchmark board. Big, clean $100 notes get the top rate.

booths.name: Siam Exchange
booths.areas: 422/3 Phaya Thai Rd · BTS National Stadium

booths.name: Airport & bank counters
booths.note: Convenience is priced in — typically 1.5–3% worse than the chains above.

atm_cash.title: Find an ATM
atm_cash.footer: The machine's fee stacks on your card's costs — the ranking above models ฿250. The ATM shows its fee before you confirm. Whatever the machine, always decline the on-screen conversion (DCC).

entries.name: Krungsri (yellow)
entries.note: Dispenses up to ฿30,000 a pull — fewer fees on big withdrawals.

entries.name: ICBC (Thai)
entries.note: Up to ฿25,000 a pull — but no Mastercard (Visa & UnionPay only).

entries.name: Kasikorn (green)
entries.note: ฿250 for Visa, ฿350 for Mastercard — the priciest since its 2025 hike.

legs.label: Binance TH
legs.acceptanceNote: Needs Thai KYC — passport + Thai bank account
legs.notes: Sell USDT/THB, withdraw to your Thai bank — the ฿20 withdrawal is the only fixed cost. Trading the pair is fee-free in practice (confirmed by real monthly use, June 2026; the standard schedule lists 0.25% and promo terms shift, so glance at the fee line on your order). Assumes you already hold USDT. Thai KYC required; capital gains via licensed Thai exchanges are income-tax-exempt 2025–2029.

legs.label: Bitkub
legs.acceptanceNote: Needs Thai KYC — passport + Thai bank account
legs.notes: Thailand's biggest THB order book. 0.25% trading fee + ฿20 withdrawal (verified bitkub.com 2026-06-11). Thai KYC required; licensed-venue capital gains are income-tax-exempt 2025–2029. Compare the live USDT/THB price before selling.

legs.label: Bitazza
legs.acceptanceNote: Needs Thai KYC — passport + Thai bank account
legs.notes: 0.25% trading fee + ฿20 withdrawal (0.001% above ฿2M; verified bitazza.co.th 2026-06-11). Thai KYC required; licensed-venue capital gains are income-tax-exempt 2025–2029. The live book price decides between venues.

legs.label: Exchange booth
legs.notes: Reputable booths charge no commission — the rate is the whole story. Large €500/€100 notes get the best rate.

legs.label: Trade Republic · ATM
legs.notes: The eurozone's travel-hero debit (verified 2026-06-11): 0% FX fee — you get Visa's network rate (~0.2–0.6% off mid, modeled 0.4%) — and free ATM withdrawals of €100+ anywhere. Thailand's ฿250 machine fee is not rebated. €5 one-time card; needs their (free) brokerage account. Runner-up: DKB, Germany-residents only.
legs.subgroupNote: Same machine, different card — the card decides the cost. Thai bank ATMs charge ฿250 a pull (modeled at ฿250; Mastercard pays ฿350 at some banks).

legs.label: Wise card · ATM
legs.notes: Wise's eurozone schedule (measured on their pricing API 2026-06-11): 0.74% EUR→THB conversion, €250/month of fee-free ATM withdrawals, then 2.69% of the excess — no fixed fee. Note: the old '€200 + €0.50 + 1.75%' numbers still floating around reviews are obsolete.
legs.subgroupNote: Same machine, different card — the card decides the cost. Thai bank ATMs charge ฿250 a pull (modeled at ฿250; Mastercard pays ฿350 at some banks).

legs.label: Revolut card · ATM
legs.notes: Revolut EU Standard (en-IE legal pages, verified 2026-06-11): 5 withdrawals or €200/month free, then 2% (min €1). Exchanging over €1,000/month adds a 1% fair-usage fee (not modeled — minor at this size), and out-of-hours conversion (Fri 5pm–Sun 6pm NY) adds 1%.
legs.subgroupNote: Same machine, different card — the card decides the cost. Thai bank ATMs charge ฿250 a pull (modeled at ฿250; Mastercard pays ฿350 at some banks).

legs.label: Your debit card · ATM
legs.notes: Modeled on a typical eurozone bank debit: ~2.2% non-EUR fee plus ~€5 per foreign withdrawal (big-bank price lists, verified 2026-06-11: ING DE 2.2%, Deutsche Bank ~1.5% min €5.99, BNP 2.9% + €3, Santander 4.5% min €3.50). Set your own bank's numbers in Setup.
legs.subgroupNote: Same machine, different card — the card decides the cost. Thai bank ATMs charge ฿250 a pull (modeled at ฿250; Mastercard pays ฿350 at some banks).

legs.label: Your credit card · ATM
legs.notes: Cash advances on eurozone credit cards: typically ~3% fee (min €5) plus interest from day one — euro-area average revolving APR is ~16.5% (ECB, 2026). Modeled assuming a 30-day payoff; set your card's fee in Setup.
legs.subgroupNote: Same machine, different card — the card decides the cost. Thai bank ATMs charge ฿250 a pull (modeled at ฿250; Mastercard pays ฿350 at some banks).

legs.label: Wise
legs.notes: Mid-market rate; the cost is the upfront fee — measured €9.46 on €1,000 (~0.95%) on 2026-06-11. SEPA-Instant-funded transfers land in minutes; a regular SEPA credit adds a day.

legs.label: Instarem
legs.notes: The quiet winner on this corridor: ~0.7% standard margin, no fee (measured live 2026-06-11; their welcome promos run even cheaper).

legs.label: Revolut
legs.notes: Revolut EU: 0.15% transfer fee (min €1.40, verified on the en-IE fee schedule 2026-06-11) plus a small measured THB spread (~0.4%). Over €1,000/month exchanged adds 1%, and weekends add 1% — convert on a weekday.

legs.label: Remitly
legs.notes: Standard (non-promo) pricing measured 2026-06-11: ~1.2% margin + €1.99 fee. The flashy headline rates are first-transfer promotions.

legs.label: Xe
legs.notes: No transfer fee above ~€500 (a small fixed fee, ~€3, applies below). The main cost is the margin — measured ~1.2% EUR→THB on 2026-06-11 (varies 0.5–2%). Bank-funded transfers take ~1–4 business days; card funding is faster.

legs.label: OFX
legs.notes: No transfer fee; margin measured ~0.7% EUR→THB on 2026-06-11 and improves on large amounts. Bank-transfer pay-in only — hence next-day delivery. Minimum transfer ~US$150 equivalent (≈€130).

atm_cash.title: Find an ATM
atm_cash.footer: The machine's fee stacks on your card's costs — the ranking above models ฿250. The ATM shows its fee before you confirm. Whatever the machine, always decline the on-screen conversion (DCC).

entries.name: Krungsri (yellow)
entries.note: Dispenses up to ฿30,000 a pull — fewer fees on big withdrawals.

entries.name: ICBC (Thai)
entries.note: Up to ฿25,000 a pull — but no Mastercard (Visa & UnionPay only).

entries.name: Kasikorn (green)
entries.note: ฿250 for Visa, ฿350 for Mastercard — the priciest since its 2025 hike.

legs.label: Exchange booth
legs.notes: Reputable booths charge no commission — the rate is the whole story. Crisp A$100 notes get the best rate.

legs.label: Macquarie debit · ATM
legs.notes: Australia's travel-hero account (verified on macquarie.com.au 2026-06-11): $0 international fees, $0 Macquarie-side ATM fees, Mastercard network rate (~0.2–0.5% off mid), no monthly fee or conditions — and 2.75% interest on the balance. Thailand's ฿250 machine fee is not rebated (no Australian bank rebates it since ING stopped in Oct 2025). Runner-up: Up Bank.
legs.subgroupNote: Same machine, different card — the card decides the cost. Thai bank ATMs charge ฿250 a pull (modeled at ฿250; Mastercard pays ฿350 at some banks).

legs.label: Wise card · ATM
legs.notes: Wise's Australian schedule (measured on their quote API 2026-06-11): ≈0.65% AUD→THB conversion, A$400/month of fee-free ATM withdrawals, then 2.69% of the excess. A$10 one-time card.
legs.subgroupNote: Same machine, different card — the card decides the cost. Thai bank ATMs charge ฿250 a pull (modeled at ฿250; Mastercard pays ฿350 at some banks).

legs.label: Revolut card · ATM
legs.notes: Revolut AU Standard (legal schedule effective Jan 2026, verified 2026-06-11): A$350 or 5 withdrawals/month free, then 2% (min A$1.50). Exchanging over A$2,000/month adds 0.5%; out-of-hours conversion (Fri 5pm–Sun 6pm NY) adds 1%.
legs.subgroupNote: Same machine, different card — the card decides the cost. Thai bank ATMs charge ฿250 a pull (modeled at ฿250; Mastercard pays ฿350 at some banks).

legs.label: Your debit card · ATM
legs.notes: Modeled on a big-4 debit card (official fee pages, verified 2026-06-11): 3–3.5% international fee (Westpac/ANZ 3%, CBA/NAB 3.5% — modeled 3%) plus A$5 per overseas withdrawal at all four. Set your bank's numbers in Setup.
legs.subgroupNote: Same machine, different card — the card decides the cost. Thai bank ATMs charge ฿250 a pull (modeled at ฿250; Mastercard pays ฿350 at some banks).

legs.label: Your credit card · ATM
legs.notes: Cash advances on Australian credit cards (big-4 official pages, verified 2026-06-11): ~3% fee (min A$3–4; ANZ caps at A$20) plus ~21.99% APR from day one, no interest-free period — modeled assuming a 30-day payoff. International fee 3–3.5% stacks on top.
legs.subgroupNote: Same machine, different card — the card decides the cost. Thai bank ATMs charge ฿250 a pull (modeled at ฿250; Mastercard pays ฿350 at some banks).

legs.label: Wise
legs.notes: Mid-market rate, 0.00% markup — the cost is the fee: A$8.99 on A$1,000 (~0.9%), verified on Wise's API 2026-06-11. PayID/OSKO-funded transfers land in seconds; cards cost more.

legs.label: Instarem
legs.notes: Best regular-user deal measured on this corridor 2026-06-11 (their own quote API): ~0.01% margin + 0.5% fee (A$5 on A$1,000 — the fee is percentage-based, so it scales). Typically lands in minutes; allow 1–2 days in edge cases. Welcome promos waive the fee entirely.

legs.label: Revolut
legs.notes: Revolut AU: 0.15% transfer fee (min A$1.44) + a small measured THB spread (~0.4%); ~0.64% all-in measured 2026-06-11. Over A$2,000/month exchanged adds 0.5%; weekends add 1%.

legs.label: Xe
legs.notes: No transfer fee — measured ~0.9% margin AUD→THB on 2026-06-11; typically lands within 24h of funding.

legs.label: OFX
legs.notes: The Australian original: no fee above A$10,000 (A$15 below), margin tiered by size — measured 0.6% via comparison referral but up to ~2.6% on small public quotes (2026-06-11). Strongest for big transfers; get their live quote.

legs.label: Remitly
legs.notes: Standard (non-promo) AUD pricing measured on Remitly's own converter 2026-06-11: ~0.6% margin + A$1.99 when paying by PayTo — the headline above-mid rates are first-transfer promos capped at A$1,000; card funding and Express cost more. Economy lands in ~3–5 business days; Express (card-funded) in minutes.

atm_cash.title: Find an ATM
atm_cash.footer: The machine's fee stacks on your card's costs — the ranking above models ฿250. The ATM shows its fee before you confirm. Whatever the machine, always decline the on-screen conversion (DCC).

entries.name: Krungsri (yellow)
entries.note: Dispenses up to ฿30,000 a pull — fewer fees on big withdrawals.

entries.name: ICBC (Thai)
entries.note: Up to ฿25,000 a pull — but no Mastercard (Visa & UnionPay only).

entries.name: Kasikorn (green)
entries.note: ฿250 for Visa, ฿350 for Mastercard — the priciest since its 2025 hike.

legs.label: Exchange booth
legs.notes: Reputable booths charge no commission — the rate is the whole story. Bring crisp ¥100 notes (¥50s get the same headline rate; ¥20s lose ~10%, ¥5s ~20% — measured 2026-06-11). Passport required. PRC cash-carry limits apply: ¥20,000 RMB / US$5,000-equivalent without paperwork.

legs.label: UnionPay debit · ATM
legs.subgroupNote: Same machine, different card — the card decides the cost. Thai bank ATMs charge ฿250 a pull (modeled at ฿250; Mastercard pays ฿350 at some banks).
legs.notes: The standard mainland rail (big-4 official schedules, verified 2026-06-11): UnionPay's network rate runs close to interbank (~0.2% modeled), your issuer charges ~1% + ¥12 per withdrawal (capped ~¥112 — ICBC/BOC/CCB alike), and Thai machines charge UnionPay the same ฿250 as Visa. SAFE caps: ¥10,000-equivalent/day, ¥100,000/year across all your cards. Set your bank's exact numbers in Setup.

legs.label: Your credit card · ATM
legs.subgroupNote: Same machine, different card — the card decides the cost. Thai bank ATMs charge ฿250 a pull (modeled at ฿250; Mastercard pays ฿350 at some banks).
legs.notes: Cash advances on dual-currency Visa/MC cards (issuer schedules, verified 2026-06-11): ~3% fee (min ¥30) plus interest at 0.05%/day (~18.25% APR) from day one, no grace period — modeled assuming a 30-day payoff. The 1.5% currency-conversion fee stacks on top (waived on 全币种 all-currency cards). Thai machines: ฿250 Visa, ฿350 Mastercard.

legs.label: Alipay Flash Remit
legs.notes: 闪速汇出 — the official RMB-native channel to a Thai bank account: 0.5% fee (min ¥100), arrives in minutes, and it does NOT consume the US$50k FX quota. Limits: ¥50,000/transfer, ¥300,000/year, 5/month; travel and study purposes only. Alipay's in-app CNY→THB rate carries a small unpublished margin (modeled ~0.4% — check the quote on screen). Verified 2026-06-11.

legs.label: Bank wire (SWIFT)
legs.notes: The big-sum route, within your US$50k annual quota (ID + purpose declaration at the bank). Bank of China's schedule as the reference (verified 2026-06-11): 0.1% handling (min ¥50, max ¥260) + ¥150 cable + ~0.4% retail FX margin; the Thai bank takes ~฿200–500 on arrival (modeled ฿350) and intermediaries can deduct $10–30. Wise, Remitly, Xe and the like cannot send CNY from the mainland.

legs.label: Western Union
legs.notes: Counter-only from the mainland and USD-intermediated: you buy USD inside the $50k quota at a partner bank (PSBC, ABC, CCB…), pay a tiered $15–40 fee, and WU converts to THB (~2% combined margin modeled). Cash pickup in minutes at Krungthai/Bangkok Bank/Thailand Post; bank deposit also available. Verified 2026-06-11.

atm_cash.footer: The machine's fee stacks on your card's costs — UnionPay pays ฿250 like Visa (Mastercard ฿350 at some banks). The ATM shows its fee before you confirm. Always decline the on-screen conversion (DCC).

entries.name: Krungsri (yellow)
entries.note: Dispenses up to ฿30,000 a pull — fewest ฿250 fees on big withdrawals.
